originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&pid_output` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= MARLIN_H false) (= PIDTEMP false) (= PID_DEBUG false))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  false)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 8c4377dd635.c is SERIAL_ECHO_START at L16 when MARLIN_H=T. This alarm is a DEAD_STORE for pid_output: when PIDTEMP=F, pid_output=1 at L11 is assigned then overwritten by pid_output=0 at L19 before ever being read — the first assignment is dead. New bug. The dead store at L11 (overwritten before read) occurs for ALL PIDTEMP=F configs regardless of MARLIN_H or PID_DEBUG. The PC {MARLIN_H=F, PIDTEMP=F, PID_DEBUG=F} unnecessarily constrains MARLIN_H and PID_DEBUG, missing other PIDTEMP=F combos where the dead store also occurs. Over-constrained.
